title: [LeakScope] 11 Android lifecycle/memory violations detected
author: MuhammadTehamsibAliTashfeen
body:
## LeakScope: Android Lifecycle & Memory Leak Violations

> **About this report:** This issue was automatically generated by **LeakScope**, a static analysis tool for Android lifecycle violations and memory leaks built on the [Soot](https://github.com/soot-oss/soot) framework. This is part of an ongoing academic research study targeting ICSE 2027. No immediate action is required — we would greatly appreciate your feedback on whether these findings are accurate.

### Summary

LeakScope detected **11 potential issue(s)** across **1 detector type(s)**:

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| 🔴 High | 0 |
| 🟡 Medium | 0 |
| 🟢 Low (improvement opportunity) | 11 |

| Detector | Count | Severity | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ViewBindingOpportunity` | 11 | 🟢 Low | Manual findViewById() calls — ViewBinding migration opportunity |

---

### Detailed Findings

#### 🟢 `ViewBindingOpportunity`
*Manual findViewById() calls — ViewBinding migration opportunity*

**Finding #1 — `Settings_Activity`**

```

Class: de.baumann.browser.activity.Settings_Activity
Type: Activity
Current Pattern: Manual view lookup
findViewById() Calls:

Benefits of View Binding:
- Eliminates boilerplate findViewById() calls
- Compile-time type safety for view references
- Reduced null pointer exceptions
- Cleaner, more maintainable code
Note: This is a code modernization suggestion, not a memory leak
```

**How to respond to this issue:**
- If a finding is a **true positive**: consider applying the recommended fix and closing this issue.
- If a finding is a **false positive**: please leave a comment explaining why — your feedback directly improves our research.
- If you have questions: reply here or open a discussion.

*This report was generated by LeakScope as part of the [ICSE 2027 research artifact](https://github.com/). Tool analyzes compiled APKs using Soot static analysis on [browser](https://github.com/woheller69/browser).*

title: Unhandled proxy URL exceptions cause fatal crash on app startup
author: fedmah
body: I set the proxy URL to a valid URL containing credentials and it caused the app to crash on start-up, requiring factory resetting the app to recover, since proxy authenticationis not implemented and the webview raised an "invalid socks proxy URL" exception which is unhandled.

title: Permit setting more exceptions on a per site basis
author: TheLNL
body: Options like desktop view, anti-fingerprinting, Dark mode, etc... con only be toggled on a per-tab or a global basis. I believe it would be useful to permit all these options to be set on a per-site basis too

title: go back not work when redirect happens
author: Kita2345
body:
URL-Redirects are part of history

If you use the non-javascript version of duckduckgo or any other site that uses URL redirects one needs to press back in a very quick sequence, making it very difficult to return to the linking page

Steps to reproduce the behaviour:

Go to https://html.duckduckgo.com/html/?q=test
Click on first link
Press back/use guesture
The page just reloads

Expected behaviour
One should return to https://html.duckduckgo.com/html/?q=test

Hard to go back to baidu.com after clicking search results

When I search something on baidu.com, like search "some" and click on the baidu baike link (https://baike.baidu.com/item/some/7777625?fr=aladdin). And press return key to go back. It hardly succeeds. Usually it just goes to the current page top

title: Soundcraft ui misbehavior
author: Kita2345
body:
Try:

https://www.soundcraft.com/ui-demo/mixer.html

it's a demo of the ui used in digital mixers.

Especially in landscape mode all the buttons on top of the site (mute, solo etc) don't work

title: History / tab close undo
author: c-o-m-m-a-n-d-e-r-d-a-t-a
body:
Love this app - thank you for making it.

Are there any plans for a history feature, or a closed tab undo? Sometimes you followed a link from some source that doesn't stick around. If you were planning to look at the tab later, and then when looking at it you accidentally press back on your device, it closes the tab and it's gone for good - you've lost the URL altogether.

Thanks!

title: [Feature Request] Support Android 6
author: maoist2009
body:
The peer close source android webview browser such as via supports android 6.0. sdk 23.

I don't think a browser requires higher sdk.

You need to change the icon to png filetype to support android 6

title: Opening new tab
author: gintasc
body: Not really a big issue, but having a, say, "+" sign to open a new tab in the "Opened Tabs" section would be more convenient and intuitive.

title: Feature Request add Bookmarks Importer
author: Chungaa
body: Why is there no bookmark importer for this browser! Where you can easily insert your bookmarks via file. Many browsers have this function, why doesn't this browser? It wouldn't be bad if you could program something like this.

title: SOCKS proxy support
author: frozen-cdn
body:
I really like what I've seen of the app so far but unfortunately there is nowhere to specify SOCKS proxy IP and port that I have found yet

Cannot set this at the system level, it should be in-app, and you could even do it by tab if you wanted since there is no additional work required or overhead when directing queries to a SOCKS server

This will allow you to use your browser with Tor.

title: FREE Browser crashes when saving bookmarklets
author: Ultrabenosaurus
body:
FREE Browser version: 3.1

In reply to #37 I was going to suggest the OP use Eruda. The main page of their documentation has a bookmarklet you can use in mobile browsers to add Eruda dev tools to any page.

https://eruda.liriliri.io/docs/

```js
javascript:(function () {var script=document.createElement('script');script.src="//cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/eruda";document.body.appendChild(script); script.onload = function () { eruda.init() } })();
```

However, after bookmarking a page and editing it to replace the URL with the JavaScript code, FREE Browser crashes, the edit is not saved, and the original bookmark is deleted. The Eruda bookmarklet works in the Fennec F-Droid browser. 

I have also tested with the following MVP bookmarklet which shows the same behavior.

```js
javascript:(function() {alert("hello world!");})();
```

title: Domain interpreted as search query
author: ghost
body: When I enter `lærepladsen.dk` in the address bar, instead of loading that site, the entered text is is used as a query for the search engine, so I have to enter `https://lærepladsen.dk` to load the site.

title: Mobile mode is not working
author: drakegao
body: Hi Team, I really like this browser and so far I found that a lot of modern websites including search.brave.com is not working on a mobile view. Many websites become the desktop view in this browser. Is there anyway to set the settings? Also it would be nice to include the brave search engine too.  I am using Android 14

title: Website rendering issues
author: Machion
body:
I am using your browser on a Moto G100 with Android 14/LineageOS 21. 
When I'm browsing certain sites, the structure, mostly pictures seem out of place or have a strange zoom. 
One example would be: I go on duckduckgo.com and do a picture search. When clicking on a picture in the grid to enlarge it, it's out of center (only partially visible).

title: Run on Android 7.0 (APK 24)
author: StevanWhite

title: Upgrade for Android Studio 2024.1.1, Gradle 8.7, JDK 17
author: StevanWhite
body:
This is just to make it easier for you, if and when you choose to update.
(Probably better sooner than later)

I have done this.  It builds and runs well, near as I can tell.
It wasn't very hard --- but there were pitfalls.

Start with the AGP Upgrade Assistant.

After recommended update to Gradle plugin 7.4.2?, 

Update all library dependencies.

Then run AGP Upgrade Assistant again to update Gradle to 8.5. 
 See a warning:

    The option setting 'android.defaults.buildfeatures.buildconfig=true' is deprecated.

Removed that line from config.

Some other suggestions do actually seem to improve build time:

    android.enableJetifier=false
    org.gradle.configuration-cache=true

At this point, build will complain:

    Cannot find symbol BuildConfig

Added to build.gradle inside `android{}`,

    buildFeatures {
        buildConfig = true
    }

At this point, it builds, and has basic functionality.  Anyway I browsed around, clicked on things, added bookmarks, etc.
(I don't know your testing procedures.)

I went on to bump the JDK versions to 17.  
Don't forget:

    kotlinOptions {
        jvmTarget = '17'
    }
(You may have to check that Gradle is using the Studio distribution jbr 17.)

Cheers!

title: crash in BannerBlock -- truncated file
author: StevanWhite
body:
Managed to build and run your app in VS 2023.3.1.

It crashes on start-up.  I debugged it, and the cause is pretty clear.

It reads the first 5k from
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mozilla/cookie-banner-rules-list/main/cookie-banner-rules-list.json
but that file is more than 5k, so it gets truncated.

In loadHosts(), this causes a JSONException, which throws a RuntimeException.

First, rather than throwing the RuntimeException, I suggest that instead you Log the error.
The app continues to run without this facility.

Second, a fix to the problem would involve correcting the code that downloads the file
(NOT just increasing the buffer size.)

Cheers!@
